content:
# 1 Thessalonians Chapter 4 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Translators:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)

---

## Verse 1

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Opening verb | "we ask you and we urge" | "we ask you and exhort you" | "we ask you and urge you" | "we ask you and urge you" — PARAKALOMEN rendered "urge" (stronger than "exhort" in modern English) |
| "how to walk" | "the how it is necessary [for] you to walk" | "how you ought to walk" | "how to live" | "how you ought to walk" — retains the walking metaphor (PERIPATEIN) that PNEUMA replaced with "live" |
| Commendation clause | "just as also you are walking" | "even as you are indeed walking" | "and you are already doing this" | "as indeed you are already doing" — blends LOGOS's formal phrasing with PNEUMA's clarity |
| "abound more" | "abound more" | "abound still more" | "do so even more" | "abound still more" — preserves PERISSEUEIN ("abound") which PNEUMA dropped |
| Sentence structure | Single long sentence with brackets | Single flowing sentence | Restructured: statement then request | Single sentence, following LOGOS structure |
| Address | "brothers" | "brothers" | "brothers and sisters" | "brothers" — see Council Notes on inclusive language |
| Name form | "Yeshua" | "Jesus" | "Jesus" | "Jesus" — English convention; EMET's "Yeshua" is a transliteration choice |

## Verse 2

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "through the Lord" | "through the Lord Yeshua" | "through the Lord Jesus" | "through the authority of the Lord Jesus" | "through the Lord Jesus" — PNEUMA's addition of "authority" is interpretive expansion |

## Verse 3

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "will of God" | "the will of the God" | "the will of God" | "What God wants" | "the will of God" — PNEUMA's paraphrase loses the noun THELEMA |
| "sanctification" | "the holiness of you" | "your sanctification" | "for you to be set apart" | "your sanctification" — HAGIASMOS is a theological term; "set apart" is interpretive paraphrase |
| "fornication" | "the fornication" | "sexual immorality" | "sexual immorality" | "sexual immorality" — PORNEIA is broader than "fornication" in modern English |

## Verse 4

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "vessel" | "the vessel of himself" | "his own vessel" | "your own body" | "his own vessel" — PNEUMA resolved the SKEUOS ambiguity; SOPHIA preserves it |
| "acquire/possess" | "acquire" | "possess" | "control" | "possess" — KTASTHAI can mean acquire or possess; PNEUMA's "control" is too free |
| "holiness" | "holiness and honor" | "sanctification and honor" | "in a way that is holy and honorable" | "sanctification and honor" — maintains consistency with v. 3 |

## Verse 5

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "passion of desire" | "passion of desire" | "the passion of desire" | "the fever of lust" | "the passion of desire" — PNEUMA's "fever of lust" is vivid but interpretive; PATHOS EPITHUMIAS is rendered directly |
| "the nations" | "the nations" | "the Gentiles" | "the nations" | "the Gentiles" — ETHNE in Pauline context means Gentiles specifically |
| "not knowing God" | "the [ones] not knowing the God" | "who do not know God" | "who do not know God" | "who do not know God" — all agree on sense |

## Verse 6

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Second verb | "defrauding" | "defraud" | "exploits" | "exploit" — PLEONEKTEIN means to take more than one's share; "exploit" is more precise than "defraud" |
| "the brother" | "the brother of him" | "his brother" | "a fellow believer" | "his brother" — PNEUMA's "fellow believer" is interpretive expansion |
| "in the matter" | "in the matter" | "in the matter" | "in this matter" | "in this matter" — minor: PNEUMA adds "this" (demonstrative implied by context) |
| "told you before" | "told you before and solemnly testified" | "told you beforehand and solemnly testified" | "warned you and made absolutely clear" | "told you beforehand and solemnly testified" — PNEUMA's rendering loses DIAMARTUROMAI |
| "avenger" | "Lord [is an] avenger" | "the Lord is an avenger" | "The Lord is the one who avenges" | "the Lord is an avenger" — PNEUMA's paraphrase is valid but less concise |

## Verse 7

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "uncleanness" | "uncleanness" | "uncleanness" | "impurity" | "uncleanness" — both are valid for AKATHARSIA; EMET/LOGOS majority |
| Preposition | "upon uncleanness but in holiness" | "for uncleanness, but in sanctification" | "to impurity but to a life set apart" | "to uncleanness but to sanctification" — normalizes prepositions; retains "sanctification" |

## Verse 8

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "rejecting" | "the [one] rejecting [this], not a man does he reject but the God" | "the one who rejects this does not reject man, but God" | "anyone who rejects this is not rejecting a human authority — they are rejecting God himself" | "the one who rejects this does not reject man but God" — PNEUMA adds "human authority" and "himself" (not in Greek) |
| Spirit clause | "the [one] also giving the Spirit of him, the Holy [One], into you" | "who also gives his Holy Spirit to you" | "the one who gives you his Holy Spirit" | "who also gives his Holy Spirit to you" — preserves KAI ("also") which PNEUMA omitted |

## Verse 9

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "brotherly love" | "the brotherly love" | "brotherly love" | "love for your fellow believers" | "brotherly love" — PHILADELPHIA is a single compound noun; PNEUMA's expansion loses the term |
| "God-taught" | "God-taught" | "taught by God" | "God himself has taught you" | "taught by God" — preserves THEODIDAKTOI while being natural English; PNEUMA over-expands |
| "need to write" | "no need [for us] to write to you" | "no need for anyone to write to you" | "you do not need anyone to write to you about that" | "no need for anyone to write to you" — PNEUMA adds "about that" |

## Verse 10

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "the whole of Macedonia" | "the whole of Macedonia" | "all Macedonia" | "throughout Macedonia" | "throughout Macedonia" — all convey the same sense; "throughout" is natural |
| Address | "brothers" | "brothers" | "dear friends" | "brothers" — PNEUMA substitutes ADELPHOI with "dear friends" |
| "abound more" | "abound more" | "abound still more" | "do so even more" | "abound still more" — preserves PERISSEUEIN |
| Conjunction | "And we urge you" | "But we exhort you" | "But we urge you" | "But we urge you" — DE is mildly adversative; "but" is appropriate |

## Verse 11

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "ambitious to be quiet" | "to be ambitious to be quiet" | "to aspire to live quietly" | "Make it your ambition to live a quiet life" | "to aspire to live quietly" — all capture the PHILOTIMEISTHAI HESUCHAZEIN paradox; LOGOS is most balanced |
| "your own affairs" | "the [things] of your own" | "your own affairs" | "your own affairs" | "your own affairs" — EMET's literalism is awkward |
| "hands of you" | "the hands of you" | "your own hands" | "your own hands" | "your own hands" — natural English |

## Verse 12

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "walk properly" | "walk properly" | "walk properly" | "conduct yourselves honorably" | "walk honorably" — blends: retains PERIPATEIN as "walk" but adopts "honorably" for EUSCHEMONOS |
| "toward the outside" | "toward the [ones] outside" | "toward those outside" | "in the eyes of outsiders" | "toward those outside" — PNEUMA's "in the eyes of" adds interpretive layer |
| "need of nothing" | "may have need of nothing" | "may have need of nothing" | "depend on no one" | "may have need of nothing" — PNEUMA changes MEDENOS (nothing) to "no one," altering the Greek |

## Verse 13

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Opening conjunction | "And we do not want" | "But we do not want" | "We do not want" | "But we do not want" — DE is present in the Greek; PNEUMA omits it |
| "ignorant/uninformed" | "ignorant" | "ignorant" | "uninformed" | "uninformed" — AGNOEIN means to not know; "uninformed" avoids the pejorative connotation of "ignorant" in modern English |
| "grieve" qualifier | "just as also the rest" | "as the rest" | "like the rest of humanity" | "as the rest" — PNEUMA adds "of humanity" (not in Greek) |
| Address | "brothers" | "brothers" | "brothers and sisters" | "brothers" — see v. 1 note |

## Verse 14

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "if we believe" | "if we believe" | "if we believe" | "Since we believe" | "if we believe" — EI with indicative is a first-class condition (assumed true), but "if" preserves the conditional form; PNEUMA's "since" is interpretive |
| "rose" | "rose" | "rose again" | "rose again" | "rose again" — ANESTE; "again" is idiomatic with resurrection language |
| "through the Yeshua/Jesus" | "through the Yeshua" (with article noted) | "through Jesus" | "through him" | "through Jesus" — PNEUMA's "through him" loses the name; EMET notes the article before IESOU in Sinaiticus |
| "so also" | "so also the God will bring" | "so also God will bring" | "we also believe that God will bring" | "so also God will bring" — PNEUMA restructures the conditional logic |
| "with him" | "with him" | "with him" | "with Jesus" | "with him" — antecedent is clear from context |

## Verse 15

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "by the word of the Lord" | "by [the] word of [the] Lord" | "by the word of the Lord" | "on the Lord's own authority" | "by the word of the Lord" — PNEUMA's expansion interprets EN LOGO KURIOU as authority claim; the literal phrase preserves the ambiguity of Paul's source |
| "the coming" | "the coming of the Lord" | "the coming of the Lord" | "the Lord comes" | "the coming of the Lord" — PAROUSIA is a noun; PNEUMA verbalizes it |
| "precede" | "shall by no means precede" | "shall by no means precede" | "will by no means go ahead of" | "shall by no means precede" — PHTHASOMEN rendered formally; PNEUMA's "go ahead of" is colloquial |
| "living/remaining" | "the [ones] living, the [ones] remaining" | "who are living, who remain" | "who are still alive and remain" | "who are living, who remain" — PNEUMA adds "still" (not in Greek, though implied) |

## Verse 16

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Opening word | "Because" (HOTI) | "For" (HOTI) | No conjunction (new sentence) | "For" — HOTI rendered as causal "for"; PNEUMA drops it |
| "command" vs. "commanding shout" | "a command" | "a commanding shout" | "a commanding shout" | "a commanding shout" — KELEUSMATI carries acoustic and authoritative force; "command" alone misses the audible dimension |
| Article before "archangel" | "the archangel" | "the archangel" | "an archangel" | "the archangel" — Sinaiticus has ARCHANGELOU with the definite sense; PNEUMA's "an" is less supported |
| "trumpet of God" | "the trumpet of God" | "the trumpet of God" | "the trumpet call of God" | "the trumpet of God" — PNEUMA adds "call" (not in Greek; SALPINGI means trumpet/trumpet blast) |
| "Christos" vs. "Christ" | "the dead in Christos" | "the dead in Christ" | "the dead in Christ" | "the dead in Christ" — EMET transliterates rather than translates CHRISTOS |

## Verse 17

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| "for the meeting" | "for [the] meeting of the Lord" | "to meet the Lord" | "to meet the Lord" | "to meet the Lord" — EIS APANTESIN rendered as purpose; EMET's nominal form is awkward in English |
| "always with the Lord" | "so always with [the] Lord we shall be" | "so we shall always be with the Lord" | "And so we will be with the Lord forever" | "so we shall always be with the Lord" — word order follows LOGOS; PNEUMA's "forever" for PANTOTE is valid but slightly different nuance |
| Tense | "shall be" | "shall be" | "will be" | "shall be" — "shall" carries the prophetic/promissory register |

## Verse 18

| Aspect | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Decision |
|--------|------|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Opening | "So then" | "Therefore" | "Therefore" | "Therefore" — HOSTE rendered as "therefore"; EMET's "so then" is slightly informal |
| "comfort/encourage" | "comfort" | "comfort" | "encourage" | "comfort" — PARAKALEITE in this context means to console those grieving (v. 13 sets the context); "encourage" is too general |

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

**Total verses with disagreements: 18 out of 18** — Every verse showed at least minor variation across the three translations.

**Most contested verses:**
- **v. 1** (7 points of divergence) — sentence structure, walking metaphor, address form
- **v. 4** (3 points) — "vessel" interpretation, verb choice, sanctification terminology
- **v. 6** (5 points) — verb rendering, relational terms, testimony language
- **v. 14** (5 points) — conditional form, "through Jesus" placement, structural rewrite
- **v. 15** (4 points) — "word of the Lord" interpretation, PAROUSIA rendering
- **v. 16** (5 points) — KELEUSMATI rendering, article before archangel, trumpet terminology

**Recurring patterns:**
1. **PNEUMA consistently adds interpretive expansions** not present in Greek: "human authority" (v. 8), "of humanity" (v. 13), "own authority" (v. 15), "trumpet call" (v. 16)
2. **EMET retains Semitic name forms** ("Yeshua," "Christos") and hyper-literal syntax with brackets
3. **LOGOS occupies the middle ground** most consistently and was most frequently adopted as the SOPHIA base
4. **PNEUMA uses inclusive language** ("brothers and sisters," "dear friends") where EMET and LOGOS retain the masculine Greek forms
5. **The eschatological section (vv. 13-18)** showed the highest density of theologically significant disagreements, particularly around the conditional in v. 14, the source-authority claim in v. 15, and the acoustic imagery in v. 16

---

— SOPHIA, Arbiter
